text: Love this -- let's shape it together and you set the pace. I can do this two ways: a QUICK interview (about 5 key questions, one at a time) or a more IN-DEPTH one (we go a little deeper, still one question at a time). Which would you like, quick or in-depth? You can switch or stop whenever you want.
in-depth
What is the title of your Signature Presentation?
CALLED: Find the Work Only You Can Do
Do you want me to provide other possible titles before I start writing?
yes — offer 2-3 alternates (e.g. "Answered", "The Unclaimed Life", "The Work Only You Can Do") before writing.
Any specific pain points to address in the avatar section?

the quietly capable mid-career professional: competent and credentialed but unseen; the ache of 'is my time ever going to come?'; exhaustion from auditioning for a life that is already theirs; feeling unclaimed, not stuck.
Key elements of your story to consider before crafting the personal-story section?

left a credentialed, established career to build the coaching practice; a first-in-family milestone; a long-ignored whisper; the leap that traded the old identity for the calling; scars that became the map; a gripping 'why'.
What do you want to teach in the transformational-teaching section? (e.g., "7 Secrets to ___", "5 Ways to ___", "Mastering the ___ Protocol", "The ___ Blueprint to ___")

The Wayfinder Blueprint — a named Blueprint organized as 3 Quests (the Purpose Quest, the Confidence Quest, the Value Quest) across 5 steps: 1) Name the Ache, 2) Read the Map You Already Carry, 3) Silence the Auditor, 4) Walk Before You're Ready, 5) Give the Gift Only You Can Give.
Do you want other possible titles for the transformational-teaching section?
yes — alternates like "The Calling Map" or "The Wayfinder's Path" are fine.
What product(s) will you offer at the end?
The Called Collective (a 12-week group mastermind) and The Wayfinder VIP Intensive (a 1-day private intensive).
Anything else to consider before I start writing?

carry the recurring motif hashtag #StillCalled through every phase; land the Quest-frame poetic fill-in-the-blank manifesto ending; keep it a purpose pitch, not a profit pitch; write in harmony with a warm, literary, conviction-forward brand voice.

Which Signature frame should carry your Transformational Teaching? (A) The Rulebook — you teach through numbered, non-negotiable Rules, each with an affirmation and a 3-step action plan, capped with a recap and a teased bonus Rule. (B) The Vault — you unlock numbered Secrets, each paired with a famous quote and its own affirmation, tied together by one running metaphor. (C) The Quest — you hand them a named Blueprint organized as Quests with steps and affirmations, closing on a poetic manifesto they finish in their own words. (D) The Original — we design a from-scratch frame around your own methodology. Not sure? Say 'show me' and I'll sketch your teaching topic in all three frames (one line each) before you pick.
quest
